blurb: If you or someone in your household is in a job search, this is worth knowing about. The City of Kennesaw, Cobb County, and CobbWorks are co-hosting the County-Wide Job Fair on May 30 from 11am to 3pm in Marietta. It's free to attend and open to the public. East Cobb has a lot of college-age kids home for the summer and adults in career transition, so an event like this can quietly be a big deal for the right person at the right time. Pass this one along if you know someone who could use it.
scoring_notes: Not a flashy pick, but functionally useful for an audience that includes teens, college students, and mid-career adults. The $118K median income means this isn't the most personally relevant event for the average reader, but the 'pass it along' angle gives it life. Uniqueness is lower since job fairs are not rare. Works best as a secondary feature or community note rather than the lead featured event.

blurb: If your Saturday mornings could use a reset, this one is worth putting on the calendar. Smith-Gilbert Gardens hosts a guided meditation session on May 23, running from 10am to 11:30am in the middle of a working botanical garden. Different meditation styles, fresh air, and no phone staring required. For East Cobb folks who are curious about meditation but have never quite made it to a studio class, doing it outside in a garden setting is a much easier first step. You'll be home before lunch.
scoring_notes: Speaks to the wellness-minded, educated, 40s-plus crowd that makes up a solid chunk of East Cobb. The garden setting elevates it above a generic yoga-studio meditation class. Uniqueness is moderate since this appears to be a recurring series, not a one-time event. Still a solid pick for the empty nester and established-family segments who have a free Saturday morning.

blurb: Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating a milestone this weekend, and it's worth the trip. Pollinator Palooza returns on May 30 for its 10th anniversary, marking a decade of their signature seasonal exhibit, A Garden with Wings. This is not a small Saturday morning program. It runs all day, 10am to 5pm, giving you plenty of time to wander at your own pace. The gardens are beautiful this time of year, and the butterfly and pollinator focus gives the whole day a sense of purpose beyond just a walk outside. Bring the grandkids, bring a neighbor, or go solo. It works for all of the above.
scoring_notes: The 10th anniversary angle and all-day format make this more of a can't-miss than a typical garden program. East Cobb's mix of established families and empty nesters both have a use for this. It's not a high-ticket splurge, which fits the casual weekend outing profile. Loses a point on audience match because the heaviest draw is families with young kids, and East Cobb skews toward teens and older.
